verdict_reasoning: HRH2 is a classical G protein-coupled receptor (GPCR) with 7 transmembrane domains. The NCBI summary explicitly states it is an 'integral membrane protein.' GPCRs have multiple extracellular loops and an N-terminal extracellular domain exposed on the outer face of the plasma membrane. HRH2 is the well-characterized target of small-molecule histamine antagonists (cimetidine, ranitidine, famotidine) that bind the receptor from the extracellular space, directly confirming surface accessibility and druggability.
